---
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

# Testing indicators and thresholds of sustainable management against multi-taxon biodiversity in Mediterranean forests

This README file provides instructions for setting up and running the
analyses for the manuscript.

## 1. System Requirements

-   **Operating System:** The software has been tested on:
    -   Windows 10 Pro Version 22H2
-   **R Version:** R version 4.5.1 or higher
-   **RStudio Version:** RStudio 2025.5.1.513 or higher
-   **Required R Packages:**
    -   `tidyverse` (version 2.0.0)
    -   `gbm` (version 2.2.2)
    -   `ggBRT` (version 0.0.0.9000)
    -   `dismo` (version 1.3.16)
    -   `cowplot` (version 1.2.0)
    -   `readxl` (version 1.4.5)
-   **Hardware Requirements:**
    -   At least 8 GB RAM

## 2. Installation Guide

### Instructions

1.  Install R from [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/).
2.  Install RStudio from
    [RStudio](https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/).
3.  Download this repository to your local machine.
4.  Open the R project file (`BRTs.Rproj`)

### Typical Install Time

-   Installation of R, RStudio, and required packages on a typical
    desktop computer should take approximately 15-30 minutes, depending
    on internet speed.

## 3. Demo

### Instructions to Run the Demo

1.  Open the R project in RStudio.
2.  Run the script located in the `scripts` folder named `BRTs.R`.
3.  The script will install the required packages, load the packages and
    the data from the `data` folder and generate outputs saved in the
    `plots` folder.

### Expected Output

-   The script will produce a series of plots that visualize the
    relationships modeled using boosted regression trees, saved as PNG
    files in the `plots` folder.
-   Summary statistics and model performance metrics will be displayed
    in the R console.

### Expected Run Time

-   The demo script should run in approximately 5 minutes.

## 4. Instructions for Use

### How to Run the Software on Your Data

1.  Place your data files in the `data` folder, ensuring they match the
    expected format.
2.  Update the file paths and variable names in the script `BRTs.R`
    located in the `scripts` folder to correspond to your dataset.
3.  Run the script in RStudio, and the analysis will proceed using your
    data.

### Reproduction Instructions (Optional)

To reproduce all quantitative results in the manuscript:

1.  Ensure that the dataset provided in the `data` folder is complete
    and unaltered.
2.  Run the main analysis script `BRTs` in the `scripts` folder.
3.  Outputs will be saved in the `plots` folder.

### Additional Notes

-   Make sure that all paths in the scripts are correctly set to match
    the structure of the project on your local machine.

## 5. Data Information

### Dataset 1: `data/dataLI.xlsx`

**Description:** This dataset includes information on Epiphytic Lichens
per sampling unit.

| Variable Name   | Description                                   | Type                      | Unit    |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------     | ------- |
| `shannon`       | Shannon diversity index of epiphytic lichens  | numeric (continuous)      | —       |
| `siteID`        | Site identifier                               | categorical               | —       |
| `CC`            | Canopy cover                                  | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `IBP_TOT`       | Index of Biodiversity Potential total score   | numeric (index, 0–50)     | —       |
| `Trems_N_Type`  | Number of Tree-Related Microhabitat types     | integer (count)           | —       |
| `Trems_Sum`     | Total abundance of Tree-Related Microhabitats | integer (count)           | —       |
| `BAXCD1–BAXCD6` | Basal area within DBH classes                 | numeric (continuous)      | m² ha⁻¹ |


### Dataset 2: `data/dataVP.xlsx`

**Description:** This dataset includes information on Vascular Plants
per sampling unit.

| Variable Name   | Description                                                     | Type                      | Unit    |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |-------------------------  | ------- |
| `PCDSU`         | Relative cover of diagnostic understorey vascular plant species | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `siteID`        | Site identifier                                                 | categorical               | —       |
| `M_CC`          | Canopy cover                                                    | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `IBP_TOT`       | Index of Biodiversity Potential total score                     | numeric (index, 0–50)     | —       |
| `Trems_N_Type`  | Number of Tree-Related Microhabitat types                       | integer (count)           | —       |
| `Trems_Sum`     | Total abundance of Tree-Related Microhabitats                   | integer (count)           | —       |
| `BAXCD1–BAXCD6` | Basal area within DBH classes                                   | numeric (continuous)      | m² ha⁻¹ |

### Dataset 3: `data/dataDA.xlsx`

**Description:** This dataset includes information on Deadwood
Arthropods per sampling unit.

| Variable Name  | Description                                      | Type / Scale          | Unit |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------- | ---- |
| `richness`     | Richness of deadwood arthropod functional groups | integer (count)       | —    |
| `diam`         | Diameter of sampled deadwood fragment            | numeric (continuous)  | cm   |
| `decay_class`  | Deadwood decay class                             | ordinal (1–5)         | —    |
| `IBP_TOT`      | Index of Biodiversity Potential total score      | numeric (index, 0–50) | —    |
| `Trems_N_Type` | Number of Tree-Related Microhabitat types        | integer (count)       | —    |
| `Trems_Sum`    | Total abundance of Tree-Related Microhabitats    | integer (count)       | —    |
| `siteID`       | Site identifier                                  | categorical           | —    |

### Dataset 4: `data/dataUA.xlsx`

**Description:** This dataset includes information on Understory
Arthropods per sampling unit.

| Variable Name   | Description                                         | Type / Scale              | Unit    |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------- |
| `richness`      | Richness of understorey arthropod functional groups | integer (count)           | —       |
| `siteID`        | Site identifier                                     | categorical               | —       |
| `M_CC`          | Canopy cover                                        | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `IBP_TOT`       | Index of Biodiversity Potential total score         | numeric (index, 0–50)     | —       |
| `Trems_N_Type`  | Number of Tree-Related Microhabitat types           | integer (count)           | —       |
| `Trems_Sum`     | Total abundance of Tree-Related Microhabitats       | integer (count)           | —       |
| `BAXCD1–BAXCD6` | Basal area within DBH classes                       | numeric (continuous)      | m² ha⁻¹ |
| `CC`            | Canopy cover                                        | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `SC`            | Shrub cover                                         | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |
| `HC`            | Herb cover                                          | numeric (proportion, 0–1) | —       |


## Contact Information

For any questions or issues, please contact Lorenzo Balducci -
[lorenzo.balducci\@uniroma1.it](mailto:lorenzo.balducci@uniroma1.it){.email}.
